37	You decompose DOWN (one node → many smaller ones). This is the complement of the dag-walker (which fills the cone UP). You do not invent new mathematics — you re-express the target's existing proof as a chain of named intermediate results.

66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

16	`bundleTransition I J` unfolded to `pullbackFreeIso (f I J) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso (M_IJ) ≪≫

18	
19	## The missing ingredient (net-new infrastructure)
20	
21	### (a) matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality
22	For `p : T ⟶ S` (`T S : Scheme.{0}`) and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`:
23	```
24	(Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
25	  = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
26	    ≫ matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom p.appTop).mapMatrix M)
27	    ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv
28	```
29	Here `p.appTop : Γ(S,⊤) ⟶ Γ(T,⊤)` is the comorphism on global sections
30	(`Scheme.Hom.appTop`). Proof route: `matrixEnd M = isoCoproduct.symm.hom ≫
31	biproduct.matrix (scalarEnd ∘ M) ≫ isoCoproduct.hom`; `Scheme.Modules.pullback p` is a left
32	adjoint, hence additive and biproduct-preserving, so it commutes with `biproduct.matrix` up
33	to the coproduct comparison; the crux is the single-entry statement
34	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a)` conjugated by the unit-pullback comparison
35	(`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the `pullbackFreeIso` at a one-element index) equals
36	`scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`. That is the genuine new lemma — `scalarEnd` naturality under
37	pullback — and lives squarely in "the diamond" (use term-mode, keep `pullbackComp`/
38	`pullbackId` opaque).
39	
40	### (b) Base-change bridge to L1's ring homs
41	The base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` induced by the projections / triple
42	transition `t'` must be identified — via `ΓSpecIso` naturality on the affine charts and the
43	way `theGlueData` / `chartTransition` are assembled from `transitionMap` — with the ring homs
44	`cocycleΘIJ` / `awayInclLeft` / `awayInclRight` over which L1 is stated. Then L1 applies to
45	the base-changed matrices produced by (a).
46	
47	## Assembly once (a)+(b) land
48	Each `pullbackBaseChangeTransport` of a `bundleTransition`, after the `pullbackComp`
49	reassociations and the three `glueData_bridge_*`/`pullbackCongr` casts cancel the
50	`pullbackFreeIso` comparisons (via (a)), becomes `matrixEnd` of a base-changed Cramer inverse
51	on `O_{V_IJK}^d`. L2 composes the first two transports to `matrixEnd ((X^J_K)⁻¹(X^I_J)⁻¹)`,
52	L1 rewrites the argument to `(X^I_K)⁻¹` (after (b)), matching the third transport.
53	
54	## Note on `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` visibility
55	L1's proof needed `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` and five matrix helpers
56	(`mul_submatrix_col`, `map_map_eq_of_comp`, `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left`,
57	`inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel`, `imageMatrix_map_eq`, `map_nonsing_inv`), all of which are
58	`private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`. They were ported verbatim into GrassmannianQuot.lean
59	as `private … '`-suffixed copies. If the Cells owner makes these `theorem`/non-private, the
60	ports can be deleted and L1 simplified.

55	\begin{definition}[Comparison of iterated pullbacks of sheaves of modules]
56	  \label{def:modules_pullbackComp}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}
58	  \mathlibok
59	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
60	  Let \(a : X \to Y\) and \(b : Y \to Z\) be morphisms of schemes. For a sheaf of
61	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}\), pulling back first along \(b\) and then
62	  along \(a\) agrees with pulling back along the composite \(b \circ a\): there is a
63	  natural isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
64	  \[
65	    a^{*}\bigl(b^{*}\mathcal{M}\bigr)
66	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
67	    (b \circ a)^{*}\mathcal{M},
68	  \]

\begin{lemma}[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
  global function, with comorphism on global sections
  \(p^{\sharp} = p_{\mathrm{appTop}} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index, i.e.\ the comparison
  \(\mathrm{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\)). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
  base-changed function:
  \[
    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
  \]
  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
  \(q\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
    def:modules_pullbackComp}
  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp} = p_{\mathrm{appTop}}\), and let
  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the index type \(\mathrm{Fin}\,d\). Then the
  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
  \[
    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
    def:modules_pullbackComp}
  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle ring homomorphisms]
  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.baseChange_bridge}
  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_the_glue_data,
    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_incl_left,
    def:gr_away_incl_right}
  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) and the triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) of the chart cover
  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}, \cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}).
  The scheme-pullback base-change map on global sections
  \(\Gamma(U^I_J, \mathcal{O}) \to \Gamma(V_{IJK}, \mathcal{O})\) induced by the first projection
  \(p^{IJ}_{IJK}\), and likewise the maps induced by the second projection and by the triple
  transition \(t'_{IJK}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}), are identified -- through
  \(\Gamma\!\mathrm{SpecIso}\) naturality on the affine charts and the assembly of
  \(\mathrm{theGlueData}\)/\(\mathrm{chartTransition}\) from \(\mathrm{transitionMap}\) -- with
  the ring homomorphisms \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}),
  \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right}) and \(\mathrm{cocycleΘ}_{IJ}\)
  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) over which the matrix cocycle
  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} is stated. Consequently the entrywise image of a Cramer
  inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) under the geometric base-change map agrees with its image under the
  corresponding \(\mathrm{cocycleΘ}/\mathrm{awayIncl}\) ring homomorphism, so
  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} applies to the base-changed matrices.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij,
    def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_away_incl_right}
  The charts \(U^I\) and their overlaps are affine, identified with the spectra of the localised
  coordinate rings; under this identification the global-sections comorphism of a morphism of
  affine charts is, by \(\Gamma\!\mathrm{SpecIso}\) naturality, the ring homomorphism that
  induces it on spectra. The triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) is the spectrum of the triple-overlap
  localisation, and the two projections and the triple transition \(t'_{IJK}\) of the glue datum
  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) are, on charts, the spectra of
  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\), \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\) and \(\mathrm{cocycleΘ}_{IJ}\)
  respectively -- this is exactly how \(\mathrm{chartTransition}\) and \(\mathrm{theGlueData}\)
  are assembled from \(\mathrm{transitionMap}\). Applying \(\Gamma\!\mathrm{SpecIso}\) naturality
  to each morphism translates its geometric base-change map on global sections into the named
  ring homomorphism, whence the entrywise images of the Cramer inverses coincide.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
  Each bundle transition is, by \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, the matrix automorphism
  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}\) of a localised Cramer inverse, conjugated by the
  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} on its source and target.
  Transporting to \(V_{IJK}\) along the relevant projection or triple transition by
  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} reassociates the iterated pullbacks
  through the pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}; the resulting
  source, middle, and target sheaves are pinned to a single free sheaf
  \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\) by the three endpoint bridges
  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}: the \emph{src} bridge identifies the common source of
  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\), the \emph{mid} bridge the
  target of \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) with the source of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\),
  and the \emph{tgt} bridge the target of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\) with that of
  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). Under these identifications the free-pullback comparisons
  cancel and each transported transition becomes
  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse:
  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_J)^{-1})\),
  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^J_K)^{-1})\),
  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_K)^{-1})\). By the linkage
  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} the composite of the first two is
  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\bigl((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\), and the matrix identity
  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} rewrites the argument as \((X^I_K)^{-1}\), so the
  composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). The bridges guarantee both sides are
  isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\), so the equality
  is well-formed.
\end{proof}

  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\), the \emph{mid} bridge the

  and the \emph{tgt} bridge the target of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\) with that of

  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}: pulling \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of a Cramer inverse back

  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge} identifies these geometric base-change maps with the ring

  over which the matrix cocycle is stated, so the three base-changed Cramer inverses are exactly

  composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). The bridges guarantee both sides are
  isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\), so the equality
  is well-formed.
